attributes.description: Alerts when `kafka.consumer_group.lag_sum > 50000` for a group+topic. Aggregate lag indicates consumer processing delay.
attributes.name: [Kafka OTel] High consumer lag by topic

investigation_guide.blob:
## Kafka high consumer lag by topic

### What fired
`kafka.consumer_group.lag_sum` exceeded 50,000 messages on a group+topic.

### Why it matters
Aggregate lag across partitions is the canonical latency proxy. Sustained growth means consumption cannot keep up with production; downstream SLAs will miss.

### Triage
1. Check if lag is concentrated on specific partitions.
2. Inspect consumer instance count and member stability.
3. Look for consumer restarts or pod evictions.

### Remediation
- Scale the consumer group.
- Fix slow consumer code paths.

### Tuning
- 50,000 is a generic starting point; tune to your throughput.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM metrics-kafkametricsreceiver.otel-*
// Topic-level lag sum (aggregate across partitions for a consumer group)
| WHERE kafka.consumer_group.lag_sum IS NOT NULL
| STATS lag_sum = MAX(kafka.consumer_group.lag_sum)
  BY attributes.group, attributes.topic
// Threshold: total lag for group on topic. Adjust for expected throughput
| WHERE lag_sum > 50000
| SORT lag_sum DESC
